questions.question: Explain the difference between signal() and sigaction() functions.
expectedPoints: signal() is the older, less reliable method
sigaction() provides more control over signal handling behavior
sigaction() allows specifying additional flags and masks
sigaction() is the preferred method in modern programs

questions.question: Describe how signal sets are used in Unix signal handling with examples.
expectedPoints: Signal sets (sigset_t) represent collections of signals
Used with functions like sigprocmask() to block/unblock signals
Functions: sigemptyset(), sigfillset(), sigaddset(), sigdelset(), sigismember()
Example: Blocking signals during critical operations
